plan.goal: Make the engine safe for first team adoption (identity, merge safety, team docs). Guard-rails: no sync-service code, no envelope-breaking changes, no content inference; SPEC.md authoritative, deviations require a logged decision.

tasks.title: Identity field: optional envelope `user` stamped at append from git config user.email (fallback: omit, never fail); strictly additive, envelope stays v1; SPEC §Envelope + decision + tests (stamps when config exists, succeeds without, mixed-log fold deterministic)

Merge safety: sofar init writes `.sofar/**/events.jsonl merge=union` to .gitattributes — idempotent, merges with existing file, never clobbers; SPEC §CLI init + tests (fresh repo gets line, double-init adds nothing, pre-existing content preserved)
TEAM section in README (<40 lines): install, one init, commit .sofar/, pull, status; why union merge is safe (append-only + fold sorts by id) and author identity from git config

payload.chose: Proceed with identity (envelope user field) and team docs under the user-directed team-readiness initiative — the CLAUDE.md "no team/identity features" guard-rail was scope law for the Fable build window (Jul 3–7) and is explicitly superseded by the user's direction for this initiative; the untouched remainder (no UI, no sync service, no telemetry, schema confined to packages/schema + projection templates) stays in force
payload.over: refusing the initiative as a guard-rail violation, or silently ignoring the guard-rail without a record
payload.because: the guard-rail's own escape hatch requires logging the conflict and surfacing it; the user's explicit instruction is the authority the guard-rail defers to, and the initiative's own guard-rails (no sync-service code, no envelope-breaking changes) keep the original intent intact

payload.chose: T1 identity as an additive OPTIONAL envelope `user` field on v1 — stamped in makeEvent (the single local minting seam) from `git config user.email`, process-cached, omitted on any failure so an append can never fail on identity; validation accepts absence and rejects only present-but-malformed values; `sofar import` appends pre-minted envelopes and therefore never restamps
payload.over: an envelope v2 with a required user field, and retrofitting identity onto existing events
payload.because: v2 would break the frozen envelope contract and force migration for zero gain over an optional field every reader already tolerates; retrofit is IMPOSSIBLE — authorship of already-appended events is unknowable after the fact (events are immutable and identity exists only at minting time), which is also why the field ships now, before first team adoption, rather than later

payload.chose: BD59 (task 12.4): resolve the next_action single-scalar collision by SURFACING, not restructuring — new fold derivation overlappingWritebacks(state) lists ended next_action-bearing sessions whose [started,ended] interval overlaps the winner's (winner = max ended; duplicates of the winner's text dropped; sequential sessions excluded), rendered as a ⚠ Parallel write-backs block directly under Next action in renderStatus (≤3 lines, 260-char clip) and sofar status (uncapped). current.next_action stays last-writer-wins (BD9); zero new event types
payload.over: modeling release/publish as a task instead of a session next-action (a workflow convention the engine cannot enforce — fixes one instance, not the class), or changing current.next_action to a per-session map (breaks the BD9 scalar contract every surface and the digest budget rely on)
payload.because: the collision is an information-loss problem, not a semantics problem: the last writer legitimately owns the scalar, but the losing overlapping threads must stay visible on resume surfaces; a read-side derivation covers every existing record retroactively and keeps the fold contract untouched

payload.note: envelope user? + core/identity.ts + validation; SPEC §Envelope updated; 8 new tests green, typecheck clean, affected suites (envelope/log/cursor/fold) all pass

payload.chose: T2 scope includes `sofar uninit`: it now strips the exact union-merge line init installs (byte-match only; a customized events.jsonl rule is user content and stays), with --purge deleting the file when the removal empties it; presence detection in init is "any line whose first token is .sofar/**/events.jsonl" so a user-customized rule wins over ours
payload.over: touching only init as the task listed, leaving uninit ignorant of .gitattributes; and exact-line presence detection in init that would append our rule below a user's conflicting one
payload.because: SPEC's uninit law is "exact inverse of init" and the Phase 8 acceptance round-trips (fresh-repo purge → only .git remains; init→uninit→init ≡ single init) fail byte-clean without the inverse; theirs-wins matches the .mcp.json customized-entry precedent; the phase-8 scenario-(b) fixture gained a pre-existing .gitattributes exactly as it already carries every other init-managed file

payload.cmd:
git commit -m "T2: sofar init writes union-merge .gitattributes rule; uninit inverts it

init merges '.sofar/**/events.jsonl merge=union' into .gitattributes —
created if missing, appended to existing content (never clobbered),
idempotent; any existing events.jsonl rule wins over ours. uninit
removes exactly our line, keeping user rules; --purge may delete the
emptied file (byte-clean round-trip law). Safe because the log is
append-only and fold replays in ulid id order — line order is
meaningless. SPEC §CLI init/uninit updated." && git log --oneline -1

payload.note: init writes/merges the union rule idempotently, uninit strips exactly our line; SPEC §CLI updated; init/uninit/phase4/phase8 suites green (88 tests)
